card.title: LocaLì
card.subtitle: Erasmus student platform · Venture pivot case study · 2025–2026

interior.headline: We killed our first startup when the financial model didn't close. Then we built this.
interior.hook: We started with CheckTheStreet — an urban safety app for students navigating Italian cities at night. We built the product, mapped the personas, designed the B2B2C model. Then we built the financial model. €2 per enrolled student, universities paying. 36-month projection: revenues couldn't cover operating costs. And the content verification problem — an open crowdsourced safety platform in the wrong hands becomes a misinformation weapon. We couldn't solve it early-stage. So we didn't defend the solution. We questioned the problem.

sections.label: The pivot
sections.body:
We asked: who experiences the information gap most intensely? Not the Italian student who partially knows the city and updates over time. The international student — arriving with zero local context, zero network, often a language barrier, for 6–12 months. The same problem, more acute, on a ticking clock.

From that question came LocaLì: not a safety tool, but the student layer of the city. Map & Discovery (finding the places locals know but tourists don't), Student Deals (aggregating the discounts scattered across ESN pages and window stickers nobody reads), Safe & Chill (crowdsourced contextual safety from students who just walked that route).

sections.label: The problem is real
sections.body:
ESNsurvey XV (2024, N=17,855): only 34% of international students actively integrate with the city they arrive in. The 66% who don't — don't lack motivation. They lack tools.

The market doesn't need to be created: 50,000 international students arrive in Italy every year (+10.7% YoY), all starting from zero. Italy is the second-largest Erasmus destination in Europe — a natural beachhead for France, Spain, Germany, Netherlands.

I was the user. I did Erasmus in Vilnius. I navigated an unknown city through fragmented WhatsApp groups, random word of mouth, and tourist circuits. That experience built the first hypothesis.

sections.label: The model
sections.body:
B2C freemium: Guest mode (free, see the map, hit a wall when you want to act) → Monthly €2.99 → Semester Pass €9.99. The conversion trigger is intentional friction at the moment of intent, not a time limit.

Market sizing built bottom-up from the student's digitally addressable wallet (€700–1,000/stay, €800 central estimate): TAM €5B (6.3M students globally), SAM €400M (Europe), SOM €40M (Italy beachhead, 50K students/year).

Live MVP shipped: localit.lovable.app

interior.credits: Entrepreneurship in Digital Age course — MSc Digital Transformation & Entrepreneurship, H-Farm College / University of Chichester, A.Y. 2025/2026.

visual_identity.rationale: Purple-tinted palette — student, urban, European. Distinct from the green (Agromercato) and dark blue (MANTA) of other pieces.

meta.your_role: Co-founder — venture design, financial modeling, market sizing, business model
